nodes.name: Schedule Trigger (Daily 08:00)
nodes.notes: Triggers daily at 08:00 UTC. In production each supplier has its own schedule entry with sourceType, url, supplier, and pickupCountry configured. For the POC, edit node-fetch-doc to switch supplier and source type.

nodes.name: Set Run Config
nodes.notes: Reads the active test case from the Form Trigger's dropdown when that's what triggered the run, otherwise falls back to ACTIVE_PRESET (used for Schedule Trigger runs). Update the 'n8n Form' string in the try block if your Form Trigger node has a different name.

nodes.name: Fetch Supplier Document (PDF / Web / Excel)
nodes.notes: Fetches the supplier T&C document. URL, sourceType, supplier, and pickupCountry are all set in the upstream 'Set Run Config' node — edit that node to switch supplier/country, not this one.

nodes.name: Extract Text — PDF
nodes.notes: Extracts text from PDF binary using n8n built-in PDF extractor. Handles multi-page PDFs. In production: OCR applied to scanned PDFs using AWS Textract before this node.

nodes.name: Pre-process & Hash Document
nodes.notes: Works identically for all three source types — PDF, Web, and Excel extractors all produce json.text and feed this node directly (no Merge node — only one branch executes per run, since Route by Source Type only fires one path, so multiple incoming connections on one input is safe). Truncates to 10,000 chars, generates MD5 hash for change detection. Supplier/country now come from Set Run Config rather than being hardcoded here.

nodes.name: Validate & Score Extraction
nodes.notes: Applies validation rules: grace period range check (0–480 min), counts low-confidence fields, determines if human review is required, flags if COB lookup is needed for TPL. In production: additional rules include cross-referencing known supplier policy patterns and checking for contradictions between fields.

nodes.name: TPL needs COB lookup?
nodes.notes: Routes to COB lookup if TPL was not explicitly stated in the supplier document. This is the expected case for most European suppliers — they reference statutory minimum rather than stating a number.

nodes.name: COB 2026 Statutory Minimum Lookup
nodes.notes: Looks up the statutory minimum TPL amount from the COB 2026 reference table for the pickup country. In production: this table is maintained as a database table, updated monthly by monitoring cobx.org for new editions. The COB disclaimer is included in every API response that references COB data.

nodes.name: Requires human review?
nodes.notes: Routes records with 2+ LOW confidence fields or validation flags to the human review queue. Records with HIGH or MEDIUM overall confidence proceed directly to the knowledge base.

nodes.name: Send to Human Review Queue
nodes.notes: Sends low-confidence extractions to the content team review queue via email. In production: this routes to a dedicated review interface (web app) where the content team can see the extracted value alongside the source document, edit if needed, and approve. No extracted field goes live in the API without passing this gate.

nodes.name: Build API-ready Record
nodes.notes: Assembles the final structured record in the schema that will be written to the T&C knowledge base and served via the aggregator API. In production: this record is written to PostgreSQL keyed by supplier × pickup_country × field_type, with full version history retained.

nodes.name: Detect Source Type
nodes.notes: Detects source type from the URL or explicit override. Outputs sourceType: 'pdf' | 'web' | 'excel'. In production this is set per supplier in a config table.

nodes.name: Route by Source Type
nodes.notes: Routes to PDF, Web, or Excel extractor based on detected source type.

nodes.name: Extract Text — Web/HTML
nodes.notes: Strips tags, scripts, nav, header, and footer; decodes common HTML entities; cleans whitespace. Reads from binary, with a UTF-8-first / Latin-1-fallback decode since supplier pages aren't consistently UTF-8 (Sixt's ES/DE terms pages serve ISO-8859-1).

nodes.name: Extract Text — Excel
nodes.notes: Extract text from Excel binary, using n8n's getBinaryDataBuffer helper rather than direct .data property access (same fix applied to Extract Text — Web/HTML, for the same reason — direct access isn't reliable across binary storage modes). For the POC: CSV/TSV and Excel-XML exports are parsed; true binary .xlsx still falls back to a placeholder string, noted as needing a real parser (openpyxl/SheetJS) in production.

nodes.name: OpenAI GPT-4o-mini — Extract T&C Fields
nodes.notes: Primary extraction using GPT-4o-mini. Temperature=0 for deterministic, consistent outputs. In production: prompt caching is used for the system prompt across all extraction calls (major cost saving). Fallback to Anthropic Claude if this node fails or confidence is LOW on all fields.

nodes.name: Build Extraction Prompt
nodes.notes: Builds the system + user prompt for the OpenAI call. Supplier hints are now conditional on data.supplier — Sixt and any other supplier without a matching hint gets the plain base prompt, with no Hertz-specific 'these fields are absent' instruction bleeding into their extraction.
